author: nflaig
content:
<@755590043632140352> might be faster to ask this here, why exactly do we wanna keep these https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5094#discussion_r3073129276, I am assuming you just mean the `else` case here which does `assert bid.parent_block_hash == parent_bid.parent_block_hash`

can you elaborate what's the rational for when something needs to be added in `on_block` vs. `process_block` vs. gossip

especially for the first two I am not sure

author: jtraglia
content: Am here & ready to chat 😁

author: nflaig
content: let's me put on the fire so it's a bit more cozy in here

author: nflaig
content: and going straight to the next comment, I agree this is wrong here https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5094#discussion_r3073215931, but what's the right approach? do we need to call `should_extend_payload`?

author: potuz
content: yeah it's the else branch, it's weird that github marked three lines, I was sure I clicked on one. In general gossip has cheap checks, and some/all of these checks need to be repeated in the handlers anyway since we sync blocks that we request out of gossip.  `on_block` has all of the forkchoice handling and it only consumes a pure function for state transition that eventually calls `process_block`, so anything that should be considered part of the STF should be in `process_block` and checks that are considered offchain should be in `on_block`. Since any node that gets a block and a state can statically perform the STF and check that this block is invalid with the check of `assert bid.parent_block_hash == parent_bid.parent_block_hash`I believe this check is better in `process_block` probably in processing the bid? Before it was in forkchoice cause we had that if-else branch there

author: nflaig
content: `process_execution_payload_bid` already has `assert bid.parent_block_hash == state.latest_block_hash`

author: potuz
content: I am not sure what the right approach is here, the wordking on the Bellatrix spec is more adapted to the current Gloas spec in which the state already determines head. I think you want to have a call to `get_head` there

author: jtraglia
content: I agree with this. My rule of thumb is that gossip performs static checks, whereas processing functions can do whatever checks they want.

author: jtraglia
content: With the exception of signature/proof verifications.

author: potuz
content: Ah this may in fact be already enough

author: nflaig
content: I removed the checks in the response to what Michael said here https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5094#discussion_r3069319758 (just fyi)

author: potuz
content: assuming the state already has the parent block applied

author: potuz
content: michael is right that after the branching is gone, then it's fine to have that in the processing function. I am uncertain about asserting against the latest block hash in the state though

author: potuz
content: if the block builds on empty we know this before doing any processing, and at this point we should know if the bid has the wrong parent hash. If we process the execution requests and modify the state's latest block hash, we also change the result of this check in the bid. I agree that we can keep everything consistent, but I think it's bug prone

author: potuz
content: it's easier to keep the check against the state latest block hash but also add the check against the parent bid parent hash in case the block builds on empty

author: nflaig
content:
so we have this gossip check
```
[REJECT] The bid's parent (defined by `bid.parent_block_root`) equals the
  block's parent (defined by `block.parent_root`).
```
isn't that the same as `assert bid.parent_block_hash == parent_bid.parent_block_hash` just for the root, why is one in gossip and the other check `on_block`

should we also add the later as a gossip rule instead, this seems like a static check to me

author: potuz
content: these can be both in gossip and in processing, but they should definitely be in processing

author: potuz
content: the thing with gossip rules is that you may not have the parent block handy

author: potuz
content: this is why I didn't like your new check for the request roots

author: potuz
content: In gossip you typically have a state around already in memory and that's good enough to validate most things. Some other things are grabbed from forkchoice that caches some info on the unfinalized blocks

author: potuz
content: but grabbing the full block from db to validate becomes costly.

author: jtraglia
content: The argument for having duplicate checks is that this matters for syncing, right?

author: potuz
content: Something I learned from Adrian Sutton I think early on is the moto that any gossip validation should be cheap eough so that an attacker cannot dos you by triggering it

author: potuz
content: correct, you cannot assume that anything that gets to processing passes the gossip validation

author: potuz
content: Anything that is required for validity has to be checked on processing

author: potuz
content: and anything that is really cheap to check may be checked in gossip

author: nflaig
content: the fact that processing needs exhaustive checks is clear to me, but what is not clear are the fork choice checks in `on_block`

author: potuz
content: yeah many of those may not be necessary

author: nflaig
content:
is that covered by
```
# [IGNORE] The block's parent has been seen (via gossip or non-gossip sources)
    # (MAY be queued until parent is retrieved)
```

author: potuz
content: We added a bunch of them to detect errors in the spec itself early on

author: potuz
content: that covers that you have it, but if you have a check that actively needs to read the block then you will need to load it from db

author: potuz
content: it's arguable if this is worth it or not

author: potuz
content: I'm uncertain if any check today requires loading the block from db

author: nflaig
content: you are talking about checkint the execution requests?

author: potuz
content: so for the execution requests in particular we would need to either 1) cache the root in forkchoice when we insert the block, or 2) request the parent block from db to check them

author: nflaig
content: no, we don't load anything from db for gossip checks, and I agree with keeping it that way

author: nflaig
content: ah you mean in `on_execution_payload`? for block we already check during processing in `process_parent_execution_payload` where we get it from `state.latest_execution_payload_bid.execution_requests_root`
